source_pr.title: test: fix ddl_test and make auto id test stable
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
Such situation always occur recently:

```
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ... obtained string = "" +
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ...     "[1]\n" +
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ...     "[100000]\n" +
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ...     "[2000001]\n"
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ... expected string = "" +
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ...     "[1]\n" +
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ...     "[100000]\n" +
[2019-10-25T05:40:05.087Z] ...     "[100001]\n"
```
This is because `mockAutoIDChange`  failpoint changed by another test.

### What is changed and how it works?

Move `TestRenameTable` to `seq_executor_test` to avoid race.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes


Side effects

 - Increased code complexity

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Release note

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: test: fix ddl_test and make auto id test stable.
fix_pattern: test: fix ddl_test and make auto id test stable

evidence: evidence: Move `TestRenameTable` to `seq_executor_test` to avoid race.
